Alternative Titles: DON; DNS; Director of Nursing Services
Primary Responsibilities: Oversees nursing services; ensures RN coverage and competencies; leads nursing operations
Evidence Snippet: CMS requires a full-time RN to serve as DON.

Primary Responsibilities: Supports DON; fills leadership gaps; helps oversee day-to-day nursing operations
Evidence Snippet: Repeated SNF postings show ADON as a standard facility leadership role.

Alternative Titles: Unit Manager; Charge Nurse; RN Supervisor
Primary Responsibilities: Supervises unit operations; covers shifts; manages bedside staff and resident flow
Evidence Snippet: CMS names charge nurse; job market shows unit manager and RN supervisor variants.

Primary Responsibilities: Provides resident care, assessments, medication/treatment oversight, supervision of aides
Evidence Snippet: SNFs must provide licensed nurses; operator pages show RNs and advanced nursing staffing.

Alternative Titles: CNA; Nurse Aide; Medication Aide; Med Tech; Medication Assistant
Primary Responsibilities: Delivers ADL care; supports resident needs; in some states administers meds under nurse supervision
Source1: CMS nurse aide rules and supervised unlicensed medication administration 7
Source2: Genesis nurse aide training and medication aide evidence 13
Evidence Snippet: Nurse aides are core SNF staff; medication aide use is state-dependent.

Alternative Titles: RNAC; MDS Nurse; LPN MDS Coordinator
Primary Responsibilities: Coordinates resident assessment process; completes/transmits MDS; supports care plans and reimbursement accuracy
Reporting Structure: Usually reports to DON or clinical/reimbursement leadership
Source2: AHCA MDS team and job evidence 15
Evidence Snippet: CMS requires RN coordination/signoff; AHCA and job postings show dedicated MDS roles.

Alternative Titles: IP; Infection Control Nurse; Infection Prevention Nurse
Primary Responsibilities: Runs IPCP; surveillance; training; antibiotic stewardship; QA committee participation
Evidence Snippet: CMS requires one or more designated Infection Preventionists.

Primary Responsibilities: Trains staff; supports competency validation; often paired with IP/quality functions
Source2: Infection-prevention training for leadership and department leads 19
Evidence Snippet: In practice this role is common and often cross-linked with education/IP duties.

Alternative Titles: Wound Care Nurse; Treatment Nurse; QA Nurse; QAPI Nurse
Primary Responsibilities: Manages wounds/treatments; audits documentation; drives quality improvement and compliance
Evidence Snippet: Frequently present in practice; exact title varies by facility.

Primary Responsibilities: Organizes and coordinates medical care; supports clinical governance; receives pharmacist irregularity reports
Source1: Pharmacy rule reference to medical director 22
Evidence Snippet: Official sources show medical director as a real SNF oversight role.

Alternative Titles: Attending Physician; Physician; Nurse Practitioner; Physician Assistant; Clinical Nurse Specialist
Primary Responsibilities: Provides orders and visits; supervises medical care; supports diet/therapy orders where allowed
Reporting Structure: Collaborates with Administrator, DON, Medical Director
Source2: Genesis facility pages with NP/physician coverage 12
Evidence Snippet: CMS explicitly names physician, PA, NP, and CNS in SNF care.

Primary Responsibilities: Reviews drug regimens monthly; consults on pharmacy services; reports irregularities
Reporting Structure: Usually consultant to attending physician, medical director, DON
Evidence Snippet: Pharmacy consultation is explicitly required, even if not directly employed.

Alternative Titles: DOR; Rehab Services Manager; Rehab Director
Primary Responsibilities: Leads therapy program; manages rehab staff and scheduling; aligns therapy with care plans and reimbursement
Reporting Structure: Usually reports to Administrator or shared therapy entity
Evidence Snippet: Official/operator and job evidence confirm dedicated rehab leadership roles.

Alternative Titles: PT; PTA; OT; COTA/OTA; SLP; Speech Therapist; Respiratory Therapist
Primary Responsibilities: Delivers therapy under physician order; supports care goals and discharge readiness
Reporting Structure: Reports to DOR or therapy leadership
Source1: CMS rehab rules and Genesis rehab pages 27
Source2: Genesis respiratory/therapy pages and therapy job evidence 28
Evidence Snippet: Therapy roles are core to modern post-acute SNF operations.

Alternative Titles: Social Worker; Director of Social Services; Social Services Designee
Primary Responsibilities: Addresses psychosocial needs; resident/family support; discharge support and advocacy
Reporting Structure: Usually reports to Administrator; collaborates with DON/MDS
Evidence Snippet: CMS requires a qualified social worker in larger facilities; providers also use director/designee variants.

Alternative Titles: Case Manager; Care Manager; Discharge Planner
Primary Responsibilities: Coordinates discharge planning; concurrent reviews; transitions and community return
Reporting Structure: Cross-functional with nursing, rehab, social services, admissions
Evidence Snippet: SNFs use discharge/care-management functions even when titles vary.

Alternative Titles: Director of Food and Nutrition Services; Dietary Manager; Food Service Manager; Certified Dietary Manager
Primary Responsibilities: Manages food and nutrition services; sanitation and food safety; participates on interdisciplinary team
Source1: CMS food and nutrition staffing rule 32
Evidence Snippet: Title variation is common, but the function is formally required.

Primary Responsibilities: Nutrition assessments; menu review; therapeutic diet support; interdisciplinary participation
Reporting Structure: May report to Administrator or consult to dietary department
Source1: CMS food and nutrition staffing rule 32
Evidence Snippet: CMS allows full-time, part-time, or consultant dietitian coverage.
